title: The Hot Tub

jokes.text: How much did you lose by? I don't even know. It was like from now to now. Now to now. Now, now, now.
jokes.setup: Jerry doing standup about Olympic silver medalists being asked about their loss
jokes.punchline: The impossibly small time measurement getting progressively smaller
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from specific to impossibly abstract. The rhythm of 'now to now' building to rapid-fire 'now, now, now' is perfectly structured.

jokes.text: If I had a pimple, I would have won.
jokes.setup: Continuing the theme of infinitesimally small Olympic margins
jokes.punchline: A pimple as the difference between gold and silver
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the timing joke, maintaining the absurdist logic with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Thirty hours later, lost in the fjord... a welcoming smile. Thank God she spotted the epaulets on my Norwegian ice-fishing vest.
jokes.setup: George reading ridiculous J. Peterman catalogue copy
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and pretentious outdoor adventure narrative
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of pretentious catalogue writing. George's deadpan delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: This catalogue is all about how to score in a foreign country.
jokes.setup: The elaborate romantic adventure story from the catalogue
jokes.punchline: George's crude reduction of the pretentious prose
jokes.explanation: Classic George - reducing something romantic to its basest interpretation with perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: I always look annoyed. When you look annoyed all the time, people think that you're busy.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking how George pretends to be busy at work
jokes.punchline: George's strategy of appearing perpetually annoyed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant workplace observation that's both universally relatable and perfectly George. The logic is twisted but undeniable.

jokes.text: Think about it.
jokes.setup: George explaining his 'looking annoyed' theory
jokes.punchline: His confident delivery of this obvious realization
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character beat - George's smugness about his 'insight' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Yeah, you do. He looks busy. He looks very busy.
jokes.setup: George's theory about looking annoyed
jokes.punchline: Jerry and Elaine immediately proving his point by observing him
jokes.explanation: Perfect validation of George's theory through immediate demonstration. Well-structured payoff.

jokes.text: Yeah, he's Trinidadian and Tobagan.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if Jean-Paul is from Trinidad and Tobago
jokes.punchline: Elaine's awkward attempt at the proper demonym
jokes.explanation: Elaine's characteristic attempt to sound worldly results in an awkward construction that sounds wrong but technically correct.

jokes.text: You'd think you'd have six alarm clocks... paying off little kids to come banging on your door.
jokes.setup: How someone could oversleep at the Olympics
jokes.punchline: Jerry's escalating backup plan ending with bribing children
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from reasonable (six alarms) to absurd (bribing kids). Classic Jerry overthinking with comic specificity.

jokes.text: What responsibility? I don't have any responsibility. / Well, you gotta wake him up. / He'll get up.
jokes.setup: Jerry saying Elaine has responsibility for the runner
jokes.punchline: Elaine's casual dismissal followed by obvious denial
jokes.explanation: Classic Elaine self-centeredness - she genuinely doesn't see why she should be responsible.

jokes.text: And the guy just took off. / Don't say anything. / Who am I gonna tell? / I know, it's just something you have to say.
jokes.setup: Elaine hearing gossip about someone
jokes.punchline: Her acknowledgment that 'don't say anything' is meaningless but necessary
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about social conventions - the ritual meaninglessness of asking people not to gossip.

jokes.text: My money's on the snooze. I bet he hit the snooze for an extra five, and it never came back on.
jokes.setup: Discussing how the runner overslept
jokes.punchline: Jerry's specific theory about snooze button failure
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's recurring theme about alarm clocks and failure points. Relatable modern anxiety.

jokes.text: Alarm clocks? No, I never use them. Don't trust them. / What do you do? / I have a mental alarm.
jokes.setup: Discussion of alarm clock reliability
jokes.punchline: Kramer's complete dismissal and bizarre alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer - rejecting modern technology for something even less reliable but delivered with complete confidence.

jokes.text: It's based on your body clock. Your body has an internal mechanism. It knows what time it is.
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining his mental alarm
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-scientific explanation delivered with authority
jokes.explanation: Kramer's confident pseudo-science. The conviction makes the absurdity funnier.

jokes.text: It's got these high-volume accusage jets... oscillating and pulsating, soothing your every aching muscle.
jokes.setup: Kramer describing his hot tub
jokes.punchline: His mispronunciation of 'acupressure' as 'accusage' plus over-the-top sales speak
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer malapropism combined with his tendency toward hyperbolic sales language.

jokes.text: Isn't that the same temperature of the coffee that scalded you? / I think it's a little cooler than that.
jokes.setup: Kramer saying 120-degree water is tolerable
jokes.punchline: Jerry's callback to Kramer being scalded, Kramer's casual response
jokes.explanation: Good callback that highlights Kramer's selective memory about pain. Jerry's dry delivery sells it.

jokes.text: He doesn't have any running water? / I don't ask those kind of questions anymore.
jokes.setup: Seeing Kramer with a bucket for his hot tub
jokes.punchline: Elaine's weary acceptance of Kramer's inexplicable situations
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of how the characters have learned to cope with Kramer's chaos. Elaine's resignation is relatable.

jokes.text: Sorry about the Olympics. / Me too.
jokes.setup: Jerry meeting the runner who overslept
jokes.punchline: The understated awkwardness of acknowledging a massive failure
jokes.explanation: The casual tone for discussing a life-altering failure is perfectly awkward. Economy of language makes it work.

jokes.text: Catalogue-writer's block? / Yeah, that's funny.
jokes.setup: Elaine can't write catalogue copy
jokes.punchline: Jerry's pun on writer's block, Elaine's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that Jerry would make. Elaine's deadpan response shows their dynamic.

jokes.text: Why separate knob! Why separate knob!
jokes.setup: Jean-Paul explaining the alarm clock volume issue
jokes.punchline: His passionate outburst about design flaw
jokes.explanation: The repetition and passion make this memorable. Jean-Paul's anguish over a design flaw that ruined his life is both funny and tragic.

jokes.text: Flight to Cleveland? / It works. / Elaine. / It works.
jokes.setup: Jerry questioning Elaine's alarm clock reliability
jokes.punchline: Elaine's stubborn insistence despite evidence of failure
jokes.explanation: Elaine's defensive repetition is character-perfect. Her refusal to acknowledge the problem is funny.

jokes.text: Singing duo, Captain and....? / Tennille!
jokes.setup: George struggling with crossword puzzle
jokes.punchline: Kramer's enthusiastic help with obvious answer
jokes.explanation: Simple setup but the characters' dynamics make it work - George struggling, Kramer eager to help.

jokes.text: There are some reps in from the Houston Astros... and I want you to show them a good time.
jokes.setup: Wilhelm saying George works too hard
jokes.punchline: Giving him a 'fun' assignment that will clearly be torture for George
jokes.explanation: The irony of George's fake stress leading to real stress. Wilhelm's obliviousness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Jean-Paul, how was your soak? A good soak? / Oh, man, very good soak. The soak of the year.
jokes.setup: Kramer asking about the hot tub experience
jokes.punchline: Jean-Paul's enthusiastic escalation to 'soak of the year'
jokes.explanation: The repetition of 'soak' and Jean-Paul's hyperbolic praise makes this memorable. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I set this thing for 20 seconds. This was for two minutes, see? / Don't say anything.
jokes.setup: Elaine burning her muffin in the microwave
jokes.punchline: Her defensive 'don't say anything' after clearly mis-setting the timer
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's theme about timing failures. Elaine's immediate defensiveness is very in character.

jokes.text: I'm not taking a soak... in that human bacteria frappe you got going there.
jokes.setup: Kramer suggesting Jerry use the hot tub
jokes.punchline: Jerry's disgusted description as 'human bacteria frappe'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant and disgusting metaphor. 'Human bacteria frappe' is pure Jerry - specific, visceral, and perfectly captures his germophobia.

jokes.text: It's like Sweden, man.
jokes.setup: Kramer describing his hot tub experience with windows open
jokes.punchline: His random cultural reference to Sweden
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer non-sequitur. The randomness and confidence of the Sweden reference is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Till this bastard over here says; 'Let's call the sons of bitches, go visit them in New York.'
jokes.setup: Meeting the Houston Astros representatives
jokes.punchline: Their casual use of profanity as terms of endearment
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag of the Astros reps' colorful language. The casual profanity as friendship is unexpected.

jokes.text: We said, 'That son of a bitch doesn't know what he's got in store for him.'
jokes.setup: Continuing the profanity-as-friendship theme
jokes.punchline: The threatening language that's meant to be friendly
jokes.explanation: Continues the gag effectively. The contrast between threatening language and friendly intent is amusing.

jokes.text: Well, maybe not as much as this bastard. / I can hold my own.
jokes.setup: Being asked if he's a big drinker
jokes.punchline: George awkwardly trying to adopt their speaking style while being modest
jokes.explanation: George's uncomfortable attempt to fit in by copying their language while still being cautious is very in character.

jokes.text: I'm saying, get the hell out of there!
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining his concerns about Elaine
jokes.punchline: His dramatic escalation to urgent advice
jokes.explanation: Jerry's increasing panic about the wake-up situation. His dramatic advice is typical Jerry overreaction.

jokes.text: They sit in a room with a big clock all night long... just waiting to make that call.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining wake-up call reliability
jokes.punchline: His vivid, slightly absurd description of wake-up call operators
jokes.explanation: Jerry's specific visualization of dedicated wake-up call operators is both logical and absurd. Good observational comedy.

jokes.text: That's how they talk. You know, everyone's a bastard or a son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about George calling people 'sons of bitches'
jokes.punchline: George's matter-of-fact explanation of baseball culture
jokes.explanation: George's anthropological observation about baseball culture. His academic tone about profanity is amusing.

jokes.text: Boy, that son of a bitch Boggs can really hit.
jokes.setup: Explaining how baseball people talk
jokes.punchline: George's example using the profanity style
jokes.explanation: Good example that extends the gag. George trying to demonstrate the concept shows his analytical nature.

jokes.text: I fell asleep in the hot tub, and the heat pump broke. Water went down to 58 degrees.
jokes.setup: Kramer appearing bundled up and shivering
jokes.punchline: The reversal from relaxing hot tub to freezing cold trap
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal of Kramer's hot tub paradise. The specific temperature and his physical state sell the comedy.

jokes.text: Your core temperature? / Here, feel my hand. Yeah, feel. / This son of a bitch is ice cold.
jokes.setup: Kramer talking about his core temperature
jokes.punchline: Jerry adopting the Astros language while reacting to Kramer's cold hand
jokes.explanation: Great callback combining the profanity gag with Kramer's physical comedy. Jerry's adoption of the language is unexpected.

jokes.text: Thirty thousand feet above your head, you bastard.
jokes.setup: Phone call from the Astros reps
jokes.punchline: Using profanity while giving their literal location
jokes.explanation: The contrast between the technical detail and the casual profanity continues the gag effectively.

jokes.text: You tell that son of a bitch no Yankee is ever coming to Houston. Not as long as you bastards are running things.
jokes.setup: George getting caught up in the phone conversation
jokes.punchline: His escalating adoption of their aggressive language style
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - getting carried away and going too far. His complete adoption of their hostility is character-perfect.

jokes.text: George! Get ahold of yourself.
jokes.setup: George's hostile phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Wilhelm's shocked reaction to George's transformation
jokes.explanation: Wilhelm's shock provides the perfect button to George's escalation. The contrast is well-structured.

jokes.text: Oh, look at that cute little bastard. You are mommy's little bastard, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Kramer talking to someone's baby
jokes.punchline: Using the Astros profanity style as baby talk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the profanity gag. The innocent baby talk combined with profanity creates perfect cringe comedy. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, come on, you son of a bitch, I'm just trying to be friendly.
jokes.setup: Being confronted about harassing tenants
jokes.punchline: Kramer's confused defense using the profanity as friendly terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer logic - he genuinely doesn't understand why friendly profanity is problematic. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Sixteen thousand BTU's.
jokes.setup: Describing his new heat pump
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific technical detail delivered with pride
jokes.explanation: Kramer's pride in technical specifications he probably doesn't understand. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Every wake-up call I make is important. You're no more important than any of our guests.
jokes.setup: Jerry stressing the importance of the wake-up call
jokes.punchline: The operator's offended professional pride
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for a minor character. The operator's dignity in the face of Jerry's anxiety is well-done.

jokes.text: I think I offended the wake-up guy. / What if he doesn't call out of spite?
jokes.setup: Jerry's interaction with the wake-up call operator
jokes.punchline: Jerry's paranoid escalation about spite
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry neurosis - turning a minor interaction into a major concern. His anxiety spiral is very in character.

jokes.text: Hey, believe me, if I had been with you there in Barcelona... you'd be polishing that medal right now.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to comfort Jean-Paul about missing the Olympics
jokes.punchline: Jerry's confident claim about his wake-up abilities
jokes.explanation: Jerry's misplaced confidence is perfectly in character. The irony makes it funnier given what's about to happen.

jokes.text: I'm exhausted. I've been on this street 1000 times. It's never looked so strange. The faces, so cold.
jokes.setup: Elaine wandering the streets
jokes.punchline: Her overwrought, catalogue-style narration of her mundane experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of catalogue writing applied to mundane experience. Elaine's breakthrough moment is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: In the distance, a child is crying. Fatherless. A bastard child, perhaps.
jokes.setup: Elaine's catalogue-style narration continuing
jokes.punchline: Her incorporation of the episode's 'bastard' theme into pretentious prose
jokes.explanation: Masterful convergence of multiple episode themes. The pretentious tone combined with crude language is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Thank God I took off my heels and put on my... Himalayan walking shoes!
jokes.setup: Elaine's continued catalogue narration
jokes.punchline: Her realization that she's found her catalogue story
jokes.explanation: Perfect story convergence. Elaine's eureka moment ties together her creative block with her actual experience. Excellent structure.

jokes.text: Jean-Paul, the electricity went out! Wake up, wake up, we've gotta go! It's 8:47! / 8:47?
jokes.setup: Jerry's confident wake-up plan
jokes.punchline: Complete failure due to power outage
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal of Jerry's confidence. The irony of his elaborate backup plans all failing due to power outage is well-executed.

jokes.text: Idiot, I trusted you!
jokes.setup: Jean-Paul realizing he's missed another race
jokes.punchline: His angry reaction to Jerry
jokes.explanation: Jean-Paul's understandable fury provides good dramatic stakes. His accusation hits Jerry's confidence.

jokes.text: Well, I guess I hit the snooze.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking what happened to Kramer's mental alarm
jokes.punchline: Kramer admitting to the very failure they discussed earlier
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that undermines Kramer's earlier confidence about mental alarms. The casual admission is very Kramer.

jokes.text: We all get a little cuckoo. I used to be like you. Berating personnel, calling managers on the field during a game.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner talking to George about cracking under pressure
jokes.punchline: His complete lack of self-awareness about his own behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Steinbrenner obliviousness. His description of past behavior as if it's past tense when he still does it is perfect.

jokes.text: I've got two words to say to you, George. Hot tub.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner's solution to George's stress
jokes.punchline: Recommending the very thing that caused problems
jokes.explanation: Good convergence of storylines. The irony of recommending the problematic solution is well-timed.

jokes.text: You got it in, but it slips out. Put it in again, it slips out again.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner talking about screwdrivers
jokes.punchline: His innocent description that sounds inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre that Steinbrenner is completely unaware of. His obliviousness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You know, we used to talk. I don't think he liked me very much.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner mentioning Mickey Mantle's death
jokes.punchline: His casual admission that Mantle probably didn't like him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Steinbrenner moment - his casual admission of being disliked by a Yankees legend is both sad and funny.

tmdb_overview: An Olympic athlete stays at Elaine's; George picks up colloquialisms from Houston Astros execs; Kramer installs a hot tub.
